title: bump-and-release suppresses all git-semver output in CI
bump-and-release suppresses all git-semver output in CI
body:
## Context

When bump-and-release runs on push events in CI, the script exits 0 with zero visible output. This makes it impossible to tell whether nothing was bumped (expected) or something went wrong.

The root cause is the `semver()` helper (line 85-87) which calls `run()` with the default `capture=True`. This passes `capture_output=True` to `subprocess.run()`, capturing all stdout from git-semver into a buffer that nobody reads. Messages like "No files changed" and "No components triggered" are silently swallowed.

Manual dispatch works because `semver("bump", bump_type)` always creates commits/tags/releases — visible side effects even though output is captured.

## Fix

Change `semver()` to pass `capture=False` so git-semver output flows through to CI logs:

```python
def semver(*args):
    return run(SEMVER_SCRIPT, *args, capture=False)
```

Both `bump-and-release` and `.vendored/pkg/git-semver/bump-and-release` (symlinked) need this fix.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Code
- [ ] `semver()` in `bump-and-release` calls `run()` with `capture=False`
- [ ] git-semver diagnostic output ("No files changed", "No components triggered", "Bumping root (patch): ...") is visible in CI logs

### Tests
- [ ] Update `tests/test_bump_and_release.py`: verify that `semver()` calls pass `capture=False` (or verify output is not suppressed)
